title: 'May next 250 years bring greater prosperity': PM Modi congratulates Trump on US Independence Day
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended warm wishes to US President Donald Trump and the American people on their 250th Independence Day. He emphasized the shared democratic values and the strength of the India-US partnership, expressing optimism for its future growth and prosperity. The US marked this significant milestone with nationwide festivities, celebrating its founding and democratic ideals.

title: Why India is planning Namo Cities—and what they could mean for Delhi-NCR
content: India is embarking on a massive urban transformation, planning to build new cities around high-speed transit to accommodate a projected population surge in the National Capital Region. These "Namo Cities," part of Regional Plan-2041, will be developed as Transit-Oriented Developments, integrating housing, jobs, and services around Namo Bharat RRTS stations.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Meta CEO Zuckerberg admits AI progress was slower than expected
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines.  Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated.  However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months.  Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: Is E20 fuel safe for your vehicle? Here's what experts say
content: Concerns surrounding E20 ethanol-blended petrol are being addressed by industry experts, who assure its safety for all vehicles, including older models. Years of rigorous scientific testing and consultations with automakers have confirmed its efficacy. This nationwide rollout aims to significantly reduce India's reliance on crude oil imports, offering a more sustainable and secure energy future while maintaining vehicle performance and environmental benefits.

title: Doctor, engineer… or something else? How India’s career dream is changing
content: In India, engineering and medicine have long been the cornerstones of middle-class aspirations, symbolizing security and respect. Yet, the landscape is transforming. With the rise of AI and digital sectors, the job market now prizes flexibility and contemporary skills over old-fashioned degrees. Professions in AI, cybersecurity, and digital marketing are emerging as attractive, rewarding alternatives to traditional paths, proving that the definition of success has evolved.

title: 'Academic jihad': BJP seeks ban on J&K textbook 'glorifying terrorists'; LG suspends 8
content: The Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir has taken decisive action by suspending officials and initiating an inquiry into school library books deemed to contain 'inappropriate content.' This decision comes after reports surfaced of a book that seemingly glorified terrorists and separatists while promoting a harmful narrative. Consequently, authors and publishers face blacklisting, and the distribution of such publications is being halted across the Union Territory.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Indra Nooyi’s ‘would never become CEO in India’ remark sparks debate
content: Indra Nooyi's remark that she could never have risen to CEO in India has sparked a heated debate among Indian professionals. While she praised the meritocratic system in the US, critics highlighted numerous successful Indian women leaders. They argue that her comments generalize and ignore the progress in India's professional landscape, suggesting a possible misunderstanding of the current business environment in India.

title: Mystery piggy banks appear on Texas shoreline, but how much cash was inside?
content: Texas beaches have been inundated with nearly 60 colourful plastic piggy banks, baffling locals and experts. These buoyant containers, originating from countries like the Dominican Republic and Guatemala, have washed ashore over the past year. While many speculate about cargo spills, experts suggest they likely entered waterways after being discarded, eventually drifting to the Texas coast.  Despite public curiosity, the piggy banks have only contained sand dollars.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to join Elon Musk, Sundar Pichai in space mission
content: Nvidia is ramping up its space computing ambitions with its first orbital data center, Space-1. The tech giant is actively hiring for a System Software Principal Architect to develop software for this new platform, designed to withstand harsh space conditions. This move signals Nvidia's commitment to orbital AI infrastructure, joining other tech giants like SpaceX and Google in exploring space-based connectivity and data centers.

title: 'Strangled with dupatta': Delhi woman kills husband in front of mother-in-law
content: A shocking incident unfolded in east Delhi's Rashid Market where a 20-year-old woman allegedly strangled her 19-year-old husband to death. The crime, reportedly committed in a fit of rage, occurred after the husband accused her of an extramarital affair and confiscated her phone. Despite his mother's attempts to intervene, the woman allegedly used her dupatta to commit the act. The accused has been arrested.

title: Tilak Varma hands Sooryavanshi historic India debut cap - Watch
content: The 15-year-old received his maiden India cap from Tilak Varma before the toss and was all smiles as he prepared for his first international appearance.

title: Historic! Sooryavanshi makes India debut, breaks Tendulkar's 36-year-old record
content: Teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi etched his name in history by becoming India's youngest-ever international cricketer at 15. His remarkable journey, fueled by a stellar IPL 2026 season where he was the highest scorer and bagged multiple awards, saw him surpass Sachin Tendulkar's debut age record. Sooryavanshi's explosive talent was evident from his U-19 World Cup heroics to his IPL contract at 13 and a record-breaking century.

title: 'Not a single brick laid': MP's 100-bed hospital fully functional on 'paper' for 6 years
content: Although a 100-bed civil hospital in Indore has been operational on paper for six years, construction has yet to commence due to persistent land acquisition issues. Curiously, 87 positions have been filled, with staff currently working at alternative facilities. The government blames land scarcity, while opposition leaders are calling for a comprehensive investigation into this apparent administrative failure.

title: 'Wedding that would never happen': Siya Goyal's 'texts' to friend surface amid murder probe
content: A viral Snapchat screenshot allegedly shows Siya Goyal requesting Aadhaar card details for non-existent wedding tickets, days after she and Chetan Chaudhary were remanded to judicial custody for the murder of Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal. The couple is accused of pushing Agarwal, Goyal's fiancé, off a cliff. Police are examining coded chats and a second recovered mobile phone, with a key witness now coming forward.

title: Why India is spending Rs 52,000 crore on the first 100 kilometres of the battlefield
content: India's defence acquisitions are shifting focus from expensive big-ticket items to weapons crucial for the initial hours of conflict. Recent approvals prioritize anti-drone systems, short-range air defence, and kamikaze drones, reflecting lessons from Operation Sindoor and preparing for a two-front contingency. This move signifies a move towards network-centric warfare, emphasizing tactical edge and survivability.

title: 'Rain only good for those in balconies': Every monsoon, same old story in Mumbai
content: Mumbai's annual monsoon test has begun with the season's first heavy downpour, exposing civic unpreparedness. Tragic incidents, including a boy's death from a fallen tree and a man drowning in an open manhole, have marred the arrival of the rains. Commuters faced disruptions, with social media buzzing about flooded roads and delayed trains, highlighting the city's ongoing struggle with its monsoon challenges.

title: India eyes $1 trillion exports milestone: Which sectors will drive growth?
content: During the April-June quarter, exports saw a 15% growth in merchandise shipments and around 11% in services exports. Which sectors have contributed to exports growth? What role has the PLI scheme played and will trade deals help expedite growth? We decode:

title: 'Became difficult to continue working': Chandrima Bhattacharya explains TMC exit
content: Chandrima Bhattacharya resigned from all Trinamool Congress posts, citing a loss of confidence from party chief Mamata Banerjee. Bhattacharya rejected claims of disloyalty, stating her decision stemmed from a phone call where Banerjee blamed her for the rebel faction's takeover of the party office. This move follows the TMC's internal crisis and a significant electoral defeat.

title: Why did Keir Starmer leave 10 Downing Street? Outgoing UK PM reveals the reason
content: Keir Starmer revealed his decision to step down as Labour leader and Prime Minister was a deeply personal one, made with his family at Chequers. He emphasized the need for his successor to navigate ongoing global and domestic challenges, defending his record of rebuilding the party. Starmer plans to remain an MP but will refrain from advising his successor, aiming to ensure the next government's success.

title: Pagdi system explained: Why is redeveloping these buildings so difficult?
content: Maharashtra's Pagdi system uniquely allows tenants to enjoy long-term occupancy by paying a one-time premium and then minimal rent. Although landlords keep ownership, tenants benefit from robust protections under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999. The challenge arises when redeveloping older properties, often complicated by disputes. In response, the government has introduced a new framework to streamline this process, incorporating specialised FSI incentives and efficient dispute resolution to facilitate redevelopment.

title: How Rs 52,000 crore worth of equipment will strengthen India's borders
content: The Defence Ministry has approved Rs 52,000 crore in upgrades, boosting military might across all domains and fostering self-reliance. Key acquisitions include advanced protection for T-90 tanks, indigenous anti-tank missiles for infantry, and high-altitude pseudo-satellites for persistent surveillance. Air defence and naval capabilities are also significantly enhanced, strengthening India's security and reducing import reliance.

title: England receive embarrassing welcome by Mexico fans ahead of FIFA World Cup R16 clash
content: England received a hostile welcome after arriving at their team hotel in Mexico City ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 match against tournament co-hosts Mexico. Hundreds of local supporters gathered outside the hotel, with many cheering for the home side while others booed as England’s team bus reached the venue.

title: 'Will fight for Punjab together': Warring plays down rift with Channi ahead of polls
content: Punjab Congress chief Amarinder Singh Raja Warring has dismissed rumours of internal party rifts, assuring that senior leaders will soon unite.  He addressed speculation about replacing him with Charanjit Singh Channi, stating that Channi remains a respected leader and meetings among party members are normal. Warring emphasized that indiscipline would not be tolerated and that unity would be showcased soon for the upcoming elections.

title: 'We lost ball, we sat back': Messi tears into Argentina after Cape Verde scare
content: Argentina sealed their place in the last 16 for the sixth straight World Cup after defeating Cape Verde in a tense round-of-32 match at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, on Saturday (IST).

title: Unmanned autonomous systems: How naval warfare is poised for a major transformation
content: India's Navy is significantly boosting its maritime security with new drone technology. Requests for tactical multi-copter surveillance drones and approval for shipborne unmanned aerial systems are underway. These advancements, alongside indigenous development of underwater vehicles, mirror global trends. Nations like the US and UK are also integrating unmanned systems, signaling a future where drones are central to naval combat, reducing risks and enhancing capabilities.

title: 'Demanded $100,000 for our release': Pak gang rape victim names deputy PM Ishaq Dar's kin
content: A cryptocurrency dispute is now central to the investigation of an alleged kidnapping and gang rape of two foreign women in Pakistan. One victim claims Raza Dar, a relative of the deputy prime minister, demanded $100,000 for their release after they were held captive and sexually assaulted. Investigators are exploring if Dar lured the women to Pakistan for a $1.5 million crypto deal, staging the abduction to appear genuine.

title: 'Absolute power corrupts': Raj Thackeray attacks BJP over 'insensitive' remarks on Mumbai deaths
content: Maharashtra Navnirman Sena chief Raj Thackeray has strongly criticized Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis and the BJP, accusing them of arrogance and shielding colleagues who made insensitive remarks after two rain-related deaths in Mumbai. Thackeray lamented the decline of Maharashtra's political culture, urging Fadnavis to demand resignations from leaders behaving "shamelessly" to uphold the state's traditions.

title: Another setback for TMC: Mamata loyalist quits party month after appointment as state chief
content: West Bengal's Trinamool Congress unit president Chandrima Bhattacharya has stepped down from all party positions amidst a wave of leaders departing the party. This development follows significant losses in the recent assembly elections and a subsequent organizational restructuring. The TMC has experienced internal divisions, with a notable split and a large contingent of Lok Sabha MPs joining a different party and aligning with the BJP-led NDA.

title: Meet Hemoprova Chutia: She embroidered the Bhagwad Gita without knowing Sanskrit
content: Assam's master weaver, Hemoprova Chutia, has been honored with the Padma Shri for her extraordinary feat of hand-embroidering the complete Bhagwad Gita, along with other devotional texts, onto fabric stretching nearly 280 feet. Remarkably, she accomplished this despite not being able to read Sanskrit or fluent in English, demonstrating immense dedication to preserving traditional weaving. Her work highlights the profound commitment and artistry involved in preserving cultural heritage.

title: 'I went up to Messi after the game. He said ... ': Vozinha after WC exit
content: After Cape Verde's dramatic 3-2 defeat to Argentina national football team, goalkeeper Vozinha revealed that Lionel Messi hugged him and said, "You are great. Your people should be proud of you." The Cape Verde veteran, who made eight saves, called the exchange one of the most memorable moments of his career.

title: Office spaces steal the spotlight as real estate investments hit six-year high
content: The second quarter of 2026 showcased explosive growth in Indian real estate, attracting a staggering $2.9 billion in institutional investments—marking a remarkable 70% increase from the previous year. With a six-year peak now reached, both domestic and international investors exhibited robust confidence, particularly in office and mixed-use properties. Notably, ADIA’s substantial $675 million investment underscored the sector's resilience amid global challenges.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: Gujarat TAT Secondary prelims result 2026 declared; scorecards available at sebexam.org
content: The State Examination Board (SEB), Gujarat, has declared the Gujarat TAT-S Preliminary Examination Result 2026 on its official portal. Candidates can access their scorecards using their confirmation number or seat number and date of birth. The board has also issued revised final answer key updates for the Gujarati Medium Common Paper and the Science and Mathematics paper. The result follows the completion of the objection process, with qualified candidates set to appear for the TAT-S Mains Examination.

title: Spouse accused of adultery can't invoke right to privacy, says SC
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that the right to privacy under Article 21 cannot shield a man from disclosing mobile call records and hotel stay details to prove adultery. Upholding the Delhi High Court's decision, the apex court stated that privacy is not absolute and can be restricted in public interest, especially when adultery is a ground for divorce under the Hindu Marriage Act.

title: Centre puts Ram temple recce operative, Hafiz Saeed's son-in-law on terror list
content: ​​According to the ministry, he carried out reconnaissance of the Ram Janmabhoomi complex in Ayodhya, the RSS headquarters in Nagpur and the Indian Oil refinery in Panipat. He is also accused of serving as the launching commander for the Lasiyakot sector and facilitating infiltration through tunnels while arranging drone deliveries of arms and ammunition into India.

title: Beyond Hormuz: What HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited means for India's energy future
content: India is bolstering its energy security following the Strait of Hormuz disruption. Prime Minister Modi inaugurated the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery, a crucial step towards self-reliance for a nation heavily reliant on oil imports. This project, alongside other development initiatives, aims to strengthen manufacturing, boost exports, and enhance resilience against future supply shocks, underscoring India's commitment to national interest and citizen welfare.

title: 'Tougher days will come': Ex-India cricketer's reality check for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Former India wicketkeeper Dinesh Karthik has lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as a rare T20 talent with the potential to shatter records. Karthik highlighted the youngster's fearless batting and ability to score freely. While acknowledging the immense potential, Karthik also advised Sooryavanshi to stay grounded and learn to manage off-field attention, which he believes will be his biggest challenge.

title: After poor Pro League campaign, India hockey coach says 'capable of beating anyone'
content: India ended the FIH Pro League season in eighth place with 19 points from 16 matches, finishing only above Pakistan, who ended without a point.

title: Punjab man set ablaze by brother-in-law while trying to bring wife home; CCTV captures horror
content: A domestic dispute turned deadly in Tarn Taran when a man, Lovepreet Singh, was allegedly set ablaze by his brother-in-law while trying to bring his wife home. Lovepreet succumbed to his injuries, and his sister-in-law, Gurjit Kaur, who attempted to save him, also died. The accused brother-in-law was injured but rescued. CCTV footage captured the horrific incident, leading to the accused's arrest and the wife's detention.

title: A final journey together: Kerala temple’s noble act gives everyone a resting place
content: A public graveyard owned by the Sree Periyattappan Mahadeva Temple at Cheruthoni in the State’s Idukki district serves as a model for religious unity, as it holds over 200 graves belonging to people from various religious communities

title: Celebratory firing murder case: Bihar BJP MLA Raju Kumar Singh gets 4-year jail
content: Singh was convicted in a 2018 case in which a woman was killed in celebratory firing, to simple imprisonment for four years and fined ₹25 lakh

title: Development works gain pace at RSR High School in Nellore
content: Inspecting the ongoing construction works on the school premises, local MP Vemireddy Prabhakar Reddy directs the engineers to finish all the amenities meant for the students on a priority basis

title: Karuvannur bank fraud case: CPI(M) leaders A.C. Moideen, K. Radhakrishnan, M.M. Varghese appear in PMLA court
content: The CPI(M) leaders, among the additional accused, had filed objections and contended that allegations in complaint were not sufficient to proceed against them for offence under Section 4 of PMLA

title: Modern command control room opened in Done to boost public safety
content: A total of 118 CCTV cameras installed at key locations across the town have been integrated into the command control room

title: Health activists seek revision of India’s essential medicines list to ensure price cap, availability
content: The Working Group on Access to Medicines and Treatments said NLEM was last notified in 2022 and contains 384 medicines, while the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines has since been revised in 2023 and again in 2025, and by the Working Group’s count now runs to 523 medicines

title: Delhi court rejects Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam bail pleas, says bound by Supreme Court order
content: Trial court holds that it has ‘no option’ but to follow the Supreme Court’s January order; defence relies on the top court’s subsequent ruling in Syed Iftikhar Andrabi v. NIA which it said reaffirmed the principle that prolonged incarceration could justify bail

title: Time to reintroduce illustrations in laws for clear understanding, less litigation: Karnataka HC
content: The law is meant for the common man and should be drafted in the simplest possible way; it should never be a puzzle, the court observes

title: JSW Group lines up ₹50,361-crore investment in Andhra Pradesh
content: The largest investment of around ₹20,350 crore in renewable energy projects are under execution in Kadapa, Sri Sathya Sai, Anantapur and Kurnool districts, followed by ₹16,350 crore in Rayalaseema Steel Plant in Kadapa district

title: YSRCP playing mind game on Amaravati, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: A capital cannot be shifted once Parliament enacts a law, he asserts, and vows to realise the ‘Swarna Kuppam’ vision, stating that it is poised to emerge as a major economic, tourism and gold mining hub

title: BMTC now accepts NCMC payments through new smart ticketing machines; adoption expected to rise
content: With the integration now in place, the NCMC issued by banks across India can be used on the city’s bus services, allowing thousands of daily commuters to use a single card for both metro and bus journeys

title: Prepare contingency plan for sowing amid delayed monsoon: Official
content: Officials told to ensure adequate stocks of seeds and fertilizers and prepare for drinking water scarcity if dry spell continues.

title: I&B Ministry steps up pressure on Telegram, seeks proactive action against piracy
content: Telegram has been accused in multiple court cases of delayed or little action in response to reports of piracy

title: Will work with T.N. government to resolve people’s issues if they approach Lok Bhavan: Arlekar
content: Speaking at a book release event, he asks people to share with him and the State government any expectations they may have of them

title: Govt. to raise ₹580 crore from UCF, UIDF for  Godavari Pushkaram projects
content: The State and Central governments will announce their financial aid later, says MA&UD Minister Narayana

title: APTF urges government to relieve transferred teachers
content: The Andhra Pradesh Teachers Federation leaders point out that 233 teachers in Nellore district, 80 in Kadapa, and 25 in Guntur, besides many others in all districts, including Subject Teachers and Secondary Grade Teachers (SGTs), are yet to be relieved

title: Bodies of two missing in boat capsize recovered from rain-swollen kole fields in Thrissur
content: Scuba team traces the bodies;  locals warn monsoon turns deceptively calm wetlands into a treacherous terrain

title: A.P. govt. focusing on upgrading secondary hospitals, says Health Minister
content: District and area hospitals, CHCs and mother and childcare hospitals are being strengthened to ensure that patients receive quality healthcare closer to homes, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: India’s P-8i maritime patrol aircraft in Hawaii for RIMPAC exercise
content: RIMPAC provides a unique training opportunity while fostering and sustaining cooperative relationships among participants critical to ensuring the safety of sea lanes and security on the world's oceans

title: KMC chief calls for cleanliness along riverbanks
content: Inspecting the catchment areas, Challa Obulesu urges officials to also raise awareness about cleanliness among local residents

title: Ex-CFO of Reliance Capital Limited held in connection with probe into Reliance ADA Group cases: CBI
content: The federal agency alleges that Amit Bapna, CFO of Reliance Capital Limited from August 2014 to December 2019, was a “key decision maker” in managing the company’s financial affairs

title: More Chinese investment good for larger relationship, says India’s envoy to China
content: Noting that trade is expanding but lopsided, Ambassador Vikram Doraiswami pitches for more Indian exports to China, especially in areas such as pharmaceuticals where India is globally competitive

title: Gujarat Police rescue missing teen from Karnataka after five-month search
content: Additional Director General of Police (Women Cell, CID Crime), Ajay Choudhary, said that Operation Milap was extended by 10 days after the completion of the month-long campaign

title: JSP leaders seek police action against YouTuber, influencer
content: Complaints lodged at multiple police stations in Vizianagaram seeking stringent action against two social media personalities over alleged remarks against Pawan Kalyan

title: Gruha Jyothi revision aims to ensure benefits reach only eligible households: Gescom chief
content: Praveen Patil Harwal, who inspected the door-to-door verification drive in Kalaburagi, says the exercise will make the scheme more transparent

title: Replicate Nalagampalli’s Net-Zero model across Kuppam, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: The sustainable development initiative can emerge as a benchmark for Andhra Pradesh, he says after inspecting households that have adopted rooftop solar panels, induction stoves, rainwater harvesting systems, kitchen composting units and terrace gardens

title: Minister pushes for industrial development, allocation of house sites at Uravakonda
content: Addressing a review meeting, Payyavula Keshav urges officials to identify suitable locations for industries, exercise utmost care regarding land acquisition for housing sites, and ensure that land is acquired only through purchase

title: India-Australia relationship has ‘never been more consequential’: PM Albanese
content: The leaders will meet in Melbourne for bilateral discussions and PM Modi will also call on Governor General Sam Mostyn AC during the visit

title: "Deeply Disturbing": Germany On Reports Of China Training Russian Troops
content: On May 20, the German daily Die Welt reported that the Chinese army had secretly trained several hundred Russian soldiers on its territory.

title: PM Modi Inaugurates Semiconductor Facility In Gujarat
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday said that India is building the entire electronics value chain from products and components to semiconductors which is the country's roadmap to 'Viksit Bharat' (developed India).

title: 'Kill Me To Stop Me': Defiant Mamata Banerjee Calls Trinamool Rebels Traitors
content: Mamata Banerjee said the Trinamool's symbol would remain with the bloc loyal to her, indicating that the rebels might face a protracted legal battle.

title: Guwahati Fake Currency Accused Injured In Police Firing While Trying To Flee
content: Ajay Biswakarma sustained bullet injuries in his leg as he tried to escape from the police on Friday night, DCP, East, TR Pegu told reporters.

title: Iran Is Preparing Thousands Of Graves As Millions Gather For Khamenei Funeral
content: According to German newspaper Die Welt, Iranian officials have drawn up emergency plans anticipating anywhere between 1,500 and 3,000 fatalities during the days-long funeral processions, which are expected to attract millions of mourners.

title: DK Shivakumar Launches Praja Seva Department To Resolve Public Grievances
content: The move is a response to the government's desire to strengthen its emphasis on service delivery and to ensure that the grievances of its citizens get timely redressal through a proper institution.

title: Ukraine Strikes Putin's Hometown, Says Russian Seizure Of Key Town A 'Lie'
content: Moscow said almost 400 Ukrainian drones attacked over a dozen Russian regions since Friday evening - including Moscow and Saint Petersburg - an attack that came after a Russian strike on Kyiv this week killed 30 people.

title: Meta Child Abuse Row: Experts Back Stricter Rules, Stronger Accountability
content: Experts state that there are several loopholes that leave children exposed to being targeted on social media companies, highlighting the need for stricter implementation of laws and enhanced scrutiny.

title: Opinion: Congress At Akal Takht: A New Chapter But Not Historical Closure
content: The Congress high command may have achieved an organisational accommodation on paper, but the real challenge will be converting this arrangement into collective leadership

title: Tech Layoffs In 1st Half Of 2026 Outpace Entire 2025, India Second-Worst Hit
content: AI-related tech layoffs worldwide touched 1.28 lakh employees by July 1, 2026, higher than the 1.25 lakh layoffs recorded during the whole of 2025.

title: J&K School Book Calls Terrorists "Great Personalities", Alleges Civil Group
content: The book, cleared under Samagra Shiksha, turns history on its head by celebrating anti-national figures as role models, the JKPF said

title: 'Wedding That Won't Happen': Siya Goyal's Snapchat Message Emerges
content: Police requested custody citing face-to-face interrogation of Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary was necessary to unravel the meaning of the conversation between them

title: 'Dehradun Now Virtually Part Of NCR': Pushkar Dhami Flaunts New Expressway
content: Pushkar Singh Dhami has become only the second chief minister in Uttarakhand to complete five years in office

title: "Avenge Blood Of Our Leader": Mourners Gather For Ali Khamenei's Funeral
content: The mourners, some in tears, made their way through strict security towards the courtyard where Khamenei's coffin was placed for people to pay their respects.

title: What's Holding Delhi Back? Housing, Water Among Key Challenges Identified
content: The issues came under the spotlight at a high-level meeting chaired by Lieutenant Governor TS Sandhu, where officials discussed a long-term development blueprint aimed at shaping the Capital's future growth.

title: UK Man Was Assaulted By Attackers. Cops Arrested Him Instead As Accused Fled
content: A clip of the footage was shared by Reform UK's Shadow Chancellor and Newark MP Robert Jenrick, who criticised the Birmingham Police in multiple posts.

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
